implemented: Read-only state generation verifies every active retention artifact path, size, and SHA-256 before mapping R0/R1-R2/R3-R7 to recovery Tier A/B/C.
Package-export payloads become canonical Tier-A package-metadata records and dataset membership binds their referenced status.
Recovery matrix CLI is dry-run by default, publishes only with --apply, is immutable and idempotent, and returns stable exit 77 for blockers.
Published matrices are self-verified and must be passing before backup use.
Tier-A backup create embeds the exact recovery matrix SHA-256; verify and restore-test can require that exact binding and reject mismatch.
State schema preflight is read-only and reports the required migration explicitly before querying unavailable tables.

validation.integrated_command: python -m pytest -q tests/test_daz_recovery.py tests/test_daz_backup.py tests/test_daz_monitoring.py tests/test_daz_storage.py tests/test_daz_scheduler.py tests/test_daz_control_plane.py tests/test_daz_runtime_worker.py

live_read_only_observation.command: python -m maskfactory.cli daz state integrity --config-root configs/daz

remaining_acceptance: Complete a governed live Tier-A backup before mutating the live queue database.
Migrate the live queue database from schema v2 through v3 and v4, then rerun integrity and the read-only recovery matrix.
Publish and bind the passing live matrix to the separate-destination backup/restore drill.
Exercise actual backup, rebuild, and optional omission routes on the clean restored root.
claim_boundary: This proves operational record derivation, immutable matrix publication, and exact backup-matrix binding in fixtures. The live DB is healthy at schema v2 but not migrated to v4, so no passing live matrix, live publication, backup/restore route, or MF-P9-12.05 completion is claimed.
